title: rubus × tegernheimensis
intro: Rubus × tegernheimensis, also known as the Tegernheim raspberry, is a hybrid plant species within the genus Rubus, producing edible fruits and leaves with potential medicinal properties. The leaves of this plant contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other compounds with antioxidant properties, which are often used to treat various health conditions. Rubus × tegernheimensis has been traditionally used to alleviate gastrointestinal issues, reduce inflammation, and support cardiovascular health due to its high levels of anthocyanins and other polyphenols. The leaves can be prepared into teas, infusions, or dried extracts, while the fruits can be consumed fresh or used in jams and preserves.
